news.title:
Crisil forecasts a 50 bps margin squeeze for India Inc. by FY27.
Airlines, paints, and chemicals sectors are expected to face the worst impact.
news.summary:
Crisil forecasts a 50 bps margin squeeze for India Inc. by FY27.
Airlines, paints, and chemicals sectors are expected to face the worst impact.

news.title:
India Inc faces uneven challenges with rising oil prices, impacting sectors like paints, chemicals, tyres, ceramics, and fertilisers.
Crisil notes limited Q4 impact due to stable months; robust balance sheets shield Indian firms from severe disruptions.
news.summary:
India Inc faces uneven challenges with rising oil prices, impacting sectors like paints, chemicals, tyres, ceramics, and fertilisers.
Crisil notes limited Q4 impact due to stable months; robust balance sheets shield Indian firms from severe disruptions.

news.title:
Crisil's Sehul Bhatt highlights LNG disruptions in West Asia, increasing prices due to conflicts impacting industries.
Industrial sectors face immediate impact, followed by refiners, with severity surpassing past global crises.
news.summary:
Crisil's Sehul Bhatt highlights LNG disruptions in West Asia, increasing prices due to conflicts impacting industries.
Industrial sectors face immediate impact, followed by refiners, with severity surpassing past global crises.

news.title:
Crisil Ratings projects gold-loan NBFCs profitability at 4.25-4.5% for FY23-24, driven by strong demand and low credit losses.
Larger NBFCs outperform mid-sized ones through better operating leverage, absorbing costs efficiently and leveraging higher branch productivity.
news.summary:
Crisil Ratings projects gold-loan NBFCs profitability at 4.25-4.5% for FY23-24, driven by strong demand and low credit losses.
Larger NBFCs outperform mid-sized ones through better operating leverage, absorbing costs efficiently and leveraging higher branch productivity.

news.title:
Private hospitals to see 14-15% revenue growth in FY27, driven by stable occupancy and high acuity care demand.
CRISIL Ratings highlights double-digit growth streak, supported by improving realisations and new bed additions.
news.summary:
Private hospitals to see 14-15% revenue growth in FY27, driven by stable occupancy and high acuity care demand.
CRISIL Ratings highlights double-digit growth streak, supported by improving realisations and new bed additions.

news.title:
C&I renewable energy capacity in India projected to reach 57 GW by FY28.
Current installed capacity expected to grow from nearly 40 GW by FY28 end.
news.summary:
C&I renewable energy capacity in India projected to reach 57 GW by FY28.
Current installed capacity expected to grow from nearly 40 GW by FY28 end.

news.title:
Gold loans see a 90% surge, driving India's retail credit boom per CRIF High Mark report.
Indians increasingly leverage gold for financing, moving beyond traditional storage.
news.summary:
Gold loans see a 90% surge, driving India's retail credit boom per CRIF High Mark report.
Indians increasingly leverage gold for financing, moving beyond traditional storage.

news.title:
Crisil projects India's consumer inflation to rise to 4.3% in FY27 from 2.5% in FY26.
The report highlights inflation estimates for April 2026 to March 2027 period.
news.summary:
Crisil projects India's consumer inflation to rise to 4.3% in FY27 from 2.5% in FY26.
The report highlights inflation estimates for April 2026 to March 2027 period.

news.title:
CRISIL Ltd approved Q4 FY25 results, recommended ₹28/share final dividend.
Reappointed MD & CEO for 3 years; Independent Director for 5 years.
news.summary:
CRISIL Ltd approved Q4 FY25 results, recommended ₹28/share final dividend.
Reappointed MD & CEO for 3 years; Independent Director for 5 years.

news.title:
Crisil Ltd reports Q4 net profit of ₹2.42B, revenue at ₹10.82B.
Crisil announces ₹28/share dividend for Dec quarter.
news.summary:
Crisil Ltd reports Q4 net profit of ₹2.42B, revenue at ₹10.82B.
Crisil announces ₹28/share dividend for Dec quarter.

news.title:
Crisil highlights diversification as key for broking firms to counter policy shifts, aiding stability amid market volatility.
Regulatory changes, including STT hike, reduced trading volumes and revenues; proprietary traders faced a 25% revenue drop.
news.summary:
Crisil highlights diversification as key for broking firms to counter policy shifts, aiding stability amid market volatility.
Regulatory changes, including STT hike, reduced trading volumes and revenues; proprietary traders faced a 25% revenue drop.

news.title:
Crisil predicts affordable housing financiers' AUM growth to moderate to 21% in FY26 from 23% in FY25.
LAP growth to slow, credit costs to rise, and profitability to remain healthy in FY26-FY27 as per Crisil.
news.summary:
Crisil predicts affordable housing financiers' AUM growth to moderate to 21% in FY26 from 23% in FY25.
LAP growth to slow, credit costs to rise, and profitability to remain healthy in FY26-FY27 as per Crisil.

news.title:
Focus on domestic fertilizer production and deep tech to boost India's fertilizer sector.
Coromandel CEO highlights customs duty rationalisation, GST fixes for cost efficiency and cash flow improvement.
news.summary:
Focus on domestic fertilizer production and deep tech to boost India's fertilizer sector.
Coromandel CEO highlights customs duty rationalisation, GST fixes for cost efficiency and cash flow improvement.

news.title:
India's manufacturing PMI rose to 55.4 in Jan from 55.0 in Dec.
Business confidence hit a 3.5-year low despite the uptick in PMI.
news.summary:
India's manufacturing PMI rose to 55.4 in Jan from 55.0 in Dec.
Business confidence hit a 3.5-year low despite the uptick in PMI.

news.title:
Crisil forecasts a 6-8% volume contraction in the cigarette industry next fiscal.
This follows additional excise duties and GST rate hikes from February 1.
news.summary:
Crisil forecasts a 6-8% volume contraction in the cigarette industry next fiscal.
This follows additional excise duties and GST rate hikes from February 1.

news.title:
Gold loan NBFCs' AUM to grow at 40% CAGR, exceeding ₹4 lakh crore by March 2027: Crisil.
Crisil Ratings highlights robust growth potential for gold loan NBFCs over this fiscal and next.
news.summary:
Gold loan NBFCs' AUM to grow at 40% CAGR, exceeding ₹4 lakh crore by March 2027: Crisil.
Crisil Ratings highlights robust growth potential for gold loan NBFCs over this fiscal and next.

news.title:
Thermal power's share in India's electricity generation is set to fall below 70% next fiscal due to rising renewable energy generation, per Crisil Ratings.
Coal-based plant utilisation may drop as renewables grow 18-20% annually. Stable cash flows support IPPs despite higher leverage from new capacity additions.
news.summary:
Thermal power's share in India's electricity generation is set to fall below 70% next fiscal due to rising renewable energy generation, per Crisil Ratings.
Coal-based plant utilisation may drop as renewables grow 18-20% annually. Stable cash flows support IPPs despite higher leverage from new capacity additions.

news.title:
RBI likely to keep rates steady in Feb 2026 amid rising inflation, says Crisil.
Crisil projects GDP growth at 6.7% next fiscal, inflation to rise to 5% in 2026-27.
news.summary:
RBI likely to keep rates steady in Feb 2026 amid rising inflation, says Crisil.
Crisil projects GDP growth at 6.7% next fiscal, inflation to rise to 5% in 2026-27.

news.title:
CRISIL reports strong headwinds for Indian merchandise exports due to US-India trade impasse and potential US levies on Russian crude.
CAD expected at 1% of GDP in FY23, rising to 1.6% by FY27; US remains top export destination.
news.summary:
CRISIL reports strong headwinds for Indian merchandise exports due to US-India trade impasse and potential US levies on Russian crude.
CAD expected at 1% of GDP in FY23, rising to 1.6% by FY27; US remains top export destination.

news.title:
India's wholesale inflation turned positive in Dec 2025 due to rising food and manufacturing prices.
Higher prices offset deflation in fuel and power, marking a second straight month of inflation rise.
news.summary:
India's wholesale inflation turned positive in Dec 2025 due to rising food and manufacturing prices.
Higher prices offset deflation in fuel and power, marking a second straight month of inflation rise.
